agent_reply: The 200 ms “settle non-connectable mode” test really was a dead end by itself. The bigger find: your build was still compiling against the stale pioarduino package `sdkconfig.h`, which still had the aggressive Bluetooth cuts enabled.

I changed [platformio.ini](</D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini>) to restore the Bluetooth stack closer to the old working config: BTDM mode back on, BLE/A2DP/SPP present again, ACL connections back to 4, bonds back to 15, BT alarms back to 50, and sync conn effective count set to 1.

I also kept the requested outbound-connect behavior in [BluetoothManager.cpp](</D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp>): ESP32-initiated HFP connect forces non-connectable/non-discoverable first, then restores connectable/non-discoverable after immediate failure or disconnect.
Important bit: I forced PlatformIO to rebuild the Arduino/IDF libs, and verified the actual included package header now has the new values:
So the next test is meaningful now: flash this build and retry ESP32-initiated connection. The previous “no improvement” run was almost certainly not testing the restored Bluetooth config at all.
